headline: Aperiodic neural activity in EEG power spectra tracks cortical excitation-inhibition balance across wake and sleep in humans.
key_takeaways: The aperiodic exponent of intracranial EEG power spectra correlates with cortical E/I balance (r = 0.74, p < 0.001)
Steeper spectral slopes during NREM sleep reflect increased inhibition, while flatter slopes during waking reflect excitation
Results replicated across 42 patients with depth electrode recordings spanning frontal, temporal, and parietal cortex
relevance: Directly validates the use of spectral parameterization for indexing E/I balance in humans, extending previous computational modeling work to clinical EEG data.
